metaTitle: HEIC to JPG Converter — Fast, Free & 100% Private (Batch)
metaDescription: Convert iPhone HEIC photos to JPG, PNG, or WebP instantly in your browser. Batch convert up to 20 files. No uploads, no limits, and 100% secure on-device processing.
introSummary: Struggling to open Apple's HEIC photos on Windows or Android? Our HEIC Converter is a professional-grade, browser-based utility designed for speed and absolute privacy. Unlike other online tools that require uploading your personal memories to a server, our 'Edge-Engine' processes every pixel locally on your device. By leveraging high-performance WebAssembly (WASM) and Web Workers, we provide a lightning-fast conversion experience without compromising your data security. Your photos stay yours, 100% of the time.

contextUsage.title: For Windows & Android Users
contextUsage.desc: Easily view and share iPhone photos on non-Apple devices by converting them to universally supported formats like JPG or PNG.

contextUsage.title: For Social Media & Web
contextUsage.desc: Optimize your Apple photos for web uploads or social media posts by converting them to highly compressed WebP format.

contextUsage.title: For Privacy-Conscious Users
contextUsage.desc: Process sensitive or personal photos without worrying about server logs or data leaks, as all conversion happens in your RAM.

howto.description: Drag and drop up to 20 HEIC or HEIF files into the conversion zone. Our tool supports high-resolution images from all iOS versions.
Select your desired format (JPG, PNG, or WebP) and adjust the quality slider to balance between file size and image clarity.
Click 'Convert'. Our local WebAssembly engine (approx. 1.5MB) loads on-demand to process your images using your computer's own CPU.
Download your converted images individually or save them all at once in a convenient, single ZIP file.

why: 100% Private & Offline-Ready — Your photos never leave your device. All processing is done in your browser's sandbox for maximum security.
Professional Batch Processing — Convert up to 20 photos simultaneously with our sequential queue system to prevent browser crashes.
WASM-Powered Performance — Utilizing a 1.5MB WebAssembly engine for near-native conversion speeds directly in the browser.
Zero Metadata Loss — Option to preserve original EXIF data, ensuring your photo dates, locations, and camera settings remain intact.
On-Demand Engine Loading — To keep our site ultra-lightweight, the conversion engine only loads when you actually need it.
Completely Free & Unlimited — No hidden costs, no watermarks, and no registration required. Just high-quality image conversion.

faq.q: Why is HEIC difficult to open on some devices?
faq.a: HEIC (High Efficiency Image Coding) is a specialized format used by Apple. While it saves space, many older Windows versions and Android apps don't support it natively yet.

faq.q: Does your tool lower the quality of my photos?
faq.a: Not unless you want it to! You can set the quality slider to 100% to maintain original resolution, or lower it to save storage space.

faq.q: Is it safe to use for personal photos?
faq.a: Absolutely. Since we use a serverless architecture, we never see, store, or transmit your files. It's the safest way to convert images online.

faq.q: Can I convert multiple photos at once?
faq.a: Yes! You can upload and batch-convert up to 20 files at a time. We use a sequential queue to ensure your browser remains stable during the process.

faq.q: What is the 1.5MB engine that loads?
faq.a: It's a WebAssembly (WASM) module that allows your browser to understand the complex HEIC format. It only loads when you start a conversion to keep the main site fast.

technicalSpecs.useCase: Native-speed processing of complex HEIC/HEIF containers
Prevents UI freezing during heavy image computation tasks
Optimized for memory stability on mobile and desktop browsers
Standardized compatibility for all web and print platforms
Compliant with strict privacy standards (GDPR / HIPAA compatible)

troubleshooting: Engine not loading? Ensure you have a stable internet connection for the initial 1.5MB engine fetch, or try refreshing the page.
File rejected? Make sure your files end in .heic or .heif. Standard JPGs or PNGs do not need this specific converter.
Browser slowing down? HEIC conversion is CPU-intensive. Try closing other tabs if you are converting many high-resolution photos at once.
proTips: Use WebP for Web! If you are a web developer, convert your HEIC photos to WebP to get the best compression for your website.
Check your iPhone settings! You can change your iPhone to always take JPGs by going to Settings > Camera > Formats and choosing 'Most Compatible'.
Download All as ZIP! When converting multiple files, use the 'Download All' button to save time instead of clicking each image.
